title: Firm Tied to Trump Donor Got No-Bid Contract to Clean Reflecting Pool
description: A White House spokeswoman said the president was not involved in selecting Greenwater Services, the business owned by a trust led by John J. Cafaro.
author: David A. Fahrenthold

title: Vance’s Defense of Iran Deal Rests on Vague and Misleading Claims
description: The vice president said the United States had leverage to dictate the outcome of the next round of negotiations. But he claimed incorrectly that Iran got no new benefit from the lifting of oil sanctions.
author: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Rebecca F. Elliott and Erica L. Green

title: Scores Fall Ill at Air Force Base After Hegseth Makes Flu Vaccine Optional
description: The defense secretary described the vaccine requirement, which he lifted in April, as an “absurd, overreaching” mandate.
author: Greg Jaffe and Maggie Haberman

title: Trump Redirects Millions From Secret Service Amid White House Construction
description: The White House insists that private donations are paying for a new ballroom, but the administration is using taxpayer money for a series of security upgrades.
author: Luke Broadwater

title: Student Cheating Is Becoming Impossible to Detect in an A.I. Era
description: Big tech companies and small start-ups are using social media to hype new tools that allow students to trick teachers and A.I. detectors.
author: Dana Goldstein

title: We Ran the Numbers. Remote Work Is Bad for Us.
description: Despite its advantages, work-from-home arrangements have significantly deepened Americans’ isolation and distress.
author: Emma Harrington and Natalia Emanuel

title: The Real Reason Trump Never Stops Talking About Voter Fraud
description: “Voter fraud” is not about fraud. It is about who votes and how.
author: Jamelle Bouie

title: Trump Administration Backs Off Plan to End Ocean Monitoring System
description: The reversal comes after the Senate passed a bipartisan bill on Wednesday to block the removal of deep-sea monitoring instruments.
author: Maxine Joselow

title: Daveigh Chase, ‘Lilo & Stitch’ Voice Actor and ‘The Ring’ Villain, Dies at 35
description: She brought the plucky Lilo to life in the popular animated film series, and unnerved viewers as the child villain Samara in the horror film “The Ring.”
author: John S.W. MacDonald

title: Supreme Court Narrows Law Banning Drug Users From Owning Guns
description: The justices sided with a Texas gun owner who faced criminal charges after admitting to marijuana use argued that a federal gun law violated the Second Amendment.
author: Abbie VanSickle

title: Knicks Accept a White House Invitation to Celebrate Title, Owner Says
description: James Dolan said he was “very proud” to bring his championship team to Washington. It would be the first N.B.A. team to visit the White House with Trump as president.
author: Santul Nerkar and Tyler Pager

title: Average U.S. Gas Price Falls Below $4 After U.S.-Iran Agreement
description: Drivers are enjoying some relief at the pump after the United States and Iran signed an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
author: Kailyn Rhone

title: Ohio’s Iconic Basket Building Is for Sale
description: The kitschy building, designed as the Longaberger basket company’s headquarters, has been a roadside attraction in Ohio for almost 30 years.
author: Daniela Gorny

title: U.S.-Iran Agreement Includes Strait of Hormuz, Lebanon and More: See Full Text of the Deal
description: New York Times reporters annotated the agreement, which a senior official disclosed on Wednesday.
author: The New York Times

title: Trump’s Deal With Iran Opens New Rifts in G.O.P.
description: Some in the president’s party were skeptical about whether the agreement he reached included adequate concessions from Iranian officials.
author: Bayliss Wagner, Robert Jimison and Tim Balk

title: Georgia Republicans Shelve Redistricting Amid Mounting Protests
description: The leaders of the Georgia legislature pulled the plug on gerrymandering away U.S. House seats held by Black Democrats just hours before a special session was to begin.
author: Rick Rojas, Sean Keenan and Alessandro Marazzi Sassoon

title: Trump Angrily Defends Iran Deal and Reveals, Again, His Obsession With Obama
description: President Trump denied that the United States would be part of a $300 billion rebuilding fund for Iran and argued that his agreement was better than the one Barack Obama struck in 2015.
author: Erica L. Green and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

title: Trump Administration to Pay $765 Million to Cancel 4 More Wind Projects
description: It’s the third such deal the Interior Department has struck to pay firms to abandon plans for offshore turbines, spending roughly $2.5 billion to get companies to abandon their wind projects.
author: Brad Plumer

title: The Real Trump Resistance Doesn’t Need a Hashtag
description: Expand the bounds of whom you are committed to. Reconsider whom you feel responsible for.
author: Julia Angwin and Ami Fields-Meyer

title: Trump Breaks Up Education Dept., Prompting Worries Over Civil Rights
description: Special education programs and the civil rights office will be moved out of the Education Department, the most aggressive move yet by the Trump administration to dismantle the agency.
author: Michael C. Bender and Dana Goldstein
